name: Genuine Texas Chili
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds chuck roast, trimmed of all fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind the beef with a coarse blade of a grinder or chop it into small pieces larger than regular ground beef.
2. Combine the meats, onions, and garlic in a large kettle or Dutch oven. Brown, stirring occasionally, for about 10 minutes.
3. Add seasonings (cumin seed, salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, and ground red pepper) and stir to mix well.
4. Shred the tomatoes by hand so that both the tomatoes and juices fall into the pot.
5. Stir in the tomato sauce and enchilada sauce. Bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat and simmer over low heat for 1 1/2 hours.
7. Add water and cook for an additional 1 1/2 hours.
8. Cool, skim off any grease, and adjust seasonings if necessary.
9. Serve hot with crackers or hard rolls.
